task_details.delay_risks: Lack of readily available information on ashfall characteristics and impact zones, delays in securing expert consultations, and difficulty in coordinating with multiple agencies.
task_details.mitigation_strategy: Establish a dedicated research team to gather and analyze ashfall data, pre-negotiate contracts with subject matter experts, and implement a centralized communication platform for all stakeholders.

task_details.delay_risks: Supply chain disruptions due to high demand for specialized equipment, logistical challenges in transporting equipment to staging areas, and budget constraints limiting the quantity and quality of equipment procured.
task_details.mitigation_strategy: Diversify equipment suppliers, establish pre-arranged transportation contracts, and explore alternative funding sources or equipment leasing options.

task_details.delay_risks: Incomplete or outdated infrastructure data, difficulty in assessing the vulnerability of specific infrastructure components, and resistance from infrastructure owners to share sensitive information.
task_details.mitigation_strategy: Conduct thorough site surveys to verify infrastructure data, develop a standardized vulnerability assessment methodology, and establish non-disclosure agreements with infrastructure owners to ensure data confidentiality.

{"goal_statement":"Preserve life through the immediate evacuation of Zone Zero (Park Interior) and Zone One (100km radius) due to a potential VEI-6 or higher eruption at the Yellowstone Caldera, and ensure continuity of operations for regional infrastructure under heavy ashfall conditions.","smart_criteria":{"specific":"The goal is to evacuate all tourists and park staff from within Yellowstone National Park and surrounding communities within a 100km radius, and to maintain essential infrastructure functionality during and after the eruption.","measurable":"Success will be measured by the number of people evacuated from Zone Zero and Zone One within the specified timeframes, the operational status of key infrastructure (hospitals, communication centers), and the absence of fatalities directly attributable to the eruption within the evacuation zones.","achievable":"The goal is achievable given the available resources, including National Park Service LE Rangers, Wyoming Highway Patrol, National Guard, and FEMA support, and by implementing contraflow traffic management and pre-staging resources.","relevant":"This goal is critical to prevent mass casualties and ensure the safety and well-being of the population in the affected areas, as well as to maintain essential services and infrastructure.","time_bound":"Evacuation of Zone Zero must be completed within 6 hours (T+6), and Zone One within 24 hours (T+24) of the initial warning.
